chunks.text: 1 Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI J. C. M. No. 38 of 2025 _____________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge 1.For hearing of CMA No.3453/2025 2.For hearing of CMA No.3454/2025 3.For hearing of main petition 4.For order on office objection a/w reply of advocate 23.02.2026 Mr. Ali Ahmed Turrabi, advocate for Applicant......... Learned Counsel for the Applicant submits that by way of ths instant JCM, the Applicant has sought rectification of the Register. Certain shares of the Respondent No.1 Company were owned by the late Zubeda Balagamwala w/o Esmail Hashim (“the Deceased”), mother of the instant Applicant. Learned Counsel submits that the Deceased’s shareholding in the Respondent No.1 Company was registered as “Jubeda Esmail” instead of her name on her Computerized National Identity Card which is “Zubeda Balagamwala”. Learned Counsel submits that due to such error in name, the legal heirs of the Deceased, such as the Applicant have been left deprived of transfer of such shareholding belonging to the Deceased, as the Respondent No. 3 has remained unable to transfer shareholding to the legal heirs of the Deceased, despite a Succession Certificate having been obtained. Learned Counsel has simply requested that the shareholding of the Deceased in the name of the Respondent No. 3 may be read as per her CNIC,1 which shows that name is to be begin with a “Z” and not with a “J”. Furthermore, a Letter dated 30.01.2026 has been issued by Respondent No. 3, giving a No- Objection for transfer of the said shares in the name of the legal heirs of the Deceased, if so directed. 1 Available at page 47

Learned Counsel has simply requested that the shareholding of the Deceased in the name of the Respondent No. 3 may be read as per her CNIC,1 which shows that name is to be begin with a “Z” and not with a “J”. Furthermore, a Letter dated 30.01.2026 has been issued by Respondent No. 3, giving a No- Objection for transfer of the said shares in the name of the legal heirs of the Deceased, if so directed. 1 Available at page 47 2 Accordingly, the same is ordered. Subject to fulfillment of all other legal formalities and codal requirements, the said shareholding of the Deceased should be read as per her CNIC, and the shareholding may accordingly be transferred to her legal heirs, if / as is otherwise permissible under the law. Once this is concluded, the Respondents should rectify their registers / record to reflect the same. Accordingly, this Application stands allowed. J U D G E Ashraf
